comments.text: This conjecture has been proved for the given cases: $G$ possessing a Sylow tower by M.A. Berger, A. Felzenbaum and A Fraenkel in [BFF87]. If the order of $G$ is divisible by few primes by Y. Ginosar and O. Schnabel in [GiSc11]. Symmetric groups and Simple groups by M.Garonzi and L. Margolis in [GaMa25].

In https://arxiv.org/pdf/1803.03569, Margolis and Schnabel have proved that Herzog-Schonheim conjecture holds for any group $G$ of order smaller than 1440. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

This is not open for abelian groups. Rather than marking it "solved", I suggest you delete the word "abelian", and then it's the Herzog--Schonheim conjecture: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herzog%E2%80%93Sch%C3%B6nheim_conjecture. The subnormal case (including the case of abelian or even nilpotent groups) was proved by Sun. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
